mechanism_narrative: A lower FEDFUNDS rate reduces the equity discount rate and supports risk appetite, so when policy stays near the effective lower bound, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month are positive if the rate remains unchanged or falls further.
failure_condition: If 20d forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline during low-rate periods, or if unchanged/falling FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating earnings expectations, so the S&P 500 should underperform over the next 20 trading days after the unemployment rate crosses a 0.3 percentage point rise threshold versus the prior three months.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show negative 20d returns more often than positive ones, or if the average 20d forward return after the trigger is non-negative for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS, HOUST, and PAYEMS all improve together, the signal indicates synchronized demand, housing turnover, and payroll income growth, which should support equity sales and earnings, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint improvement condition fails to outperform single-series signals, or if realized 60d forward returns are negative in more than 50% of jointly positive instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in INDPRO indicates industrial output recovery and stronger cyclical production momentum, which should lift equity returns over the next week as investors reprice near-term growth expectations upward.
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO breakout episodes do not produce above-baseline 5d returns, or if the mean 5d forward return after the trigger is below zero across a rolling 12-month evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are deeply negative and financial conditions are easy, so the S&P 500 should deliver positive 20-day forward returns as liquidity dominates valuation pressure.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined inflation-above-policy condition does not raise the probability of positive 20d returns above 60%, or if drawdowns dominate in these episodes over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: In a renewed labor-market shock, a rising UNRATE paired with falling PAYEMS signals income loss and weaker consumption, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month after the joint stress condition appears.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration condition does not improve bearish hit rate versus UNRATE alone, or if positive 20d returns occur in at least 45% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in GDPC1 indicates stronger real demand and profit growth expectations, supporting S&P 500 performance over the next quarter because valuation multiples can hold when output growth is expanding.
failure_condition: Reject if positive quarterly GDP growth does not correspond to above-median 60d forward returns, or if the signal has negative rank correlation with forward returns over any 4-quarter rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a manufacturing rebound regime, rising INDPRO combined with flat or rising HOUST implies production broadening beyond housing weakness, which should support S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as cyclicals lead the market.
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO-HOUST combination fails to outperform INDPRO alone, or if the combined signal is followed by negative 20d returns in more than 55% of matched cases.
